explanation_used:
1. **Opening statement**: This neuron strongly responds to **text segments that reference formal institutional actions, announcements, or reporting** (such as financial ownership disclosures, political agency statements, media attribution lines, or legal/court proceedings).

2. **Domain coverage**: The pattern appears across news and formal reporting contexts in multiple languages (English, Arabic, Spanish, Italian, Slovak, etc.), covering domains like finance (stock holdings), politics (government decisions, leadership statements), legal cases, media sourcing ("Forbes contributor"), and institutional press releases. It activates on text that often includes proper nouns (organizations, leaders, agencies) and structured, declarative information.

3. **Contrast with low-activation examples**: The high-activation texts consistently contain references to specific institutions (e.g., "Leavell Investment Management Inc.," "North Korean leader," "party's interim president Sonia Gandhi," "National Trust," "court appointed lawyer") performing or reporting defined actions. Low-activation examples are more often simple event reports ("seismic event," "tunnel will be closed"), generic statements, or headlines lacking this layer of institutional agency or formal attribution.

4. **Closing interpretation**: The neuron appears to detect formal, source-anchored reporting language where an institution, figure, or organization is centrally involved in the statement. It functions as a filter for text that resembles structured news dispatches, official communications, or documented proceedings rather than raw event descriptions or informal updates.

results.actual_text: número universal de hoy es el seis. Tu regente, el planetoide Plutón está en Capricornio. Buenas nuevas, Escorpi
results.plain_text: Noticiero astrológico: la Luna está en Leo y todos los planetas están directos excepto Mercurio que sigue retrógrado en Piscis. El número universal de hoy es el seis. Tu regente, el planetoide Plutón está en Capricornio. Buenas nuevas, Escorpión. El escenario en el que se están desarrollando los acontecimientos augura lo mejor para ti sobre todo en cuestiones de dinero y proyectos laborales. Es tu momento de la entrevista de trabajo, cambio de casa y empleo. También es positivo en tu vida afectiva, pero cuidado porque debido a tu interés en otras cuestiones podrías estar descuidando tu hogar y no prestar atención a tu pareja y familia como debes. Durante este ciclo más que en otros debes medir bien tus palabras para no herir a quien te aman. Ritual para atrapar a la pareja que tienes  Amor  Estás en un momento ideal para la reconciliación si has estado alejado de una persona amada debido a malos entendidos o peleas sin sentido ni razón. Usa tu creatividad e ingenio y haz lo que tu corazón te está indicando, Escorpión. Salud  Escucha tu organismo. Tu cuerpo emite señales y debes atenderlas bien. Te pondrán en aviso para que te preocupes más por tu salud. Cualquier problema físico, si es tratado a tiempo te evitará mayores complicaciones. Trabajo  Para poner a funcionar un plan de trabajo nuevo en tu empleo vas a requerir la colaboración de los demás. No trates de imponer tus ideas, sino convencerlos con argumentos. Si no trabajas en equipo no lograrás nada y durante estos días, hasta el 28 de marzo, debido a la acción retrógrada de Mercurio las gestiones caminan con lentitud. Escorpión en el sexo: así es con otros signos  Dinero y fortuna  Deja atrás los temores y aplica audazmente tus ideas económicas. El dinero y la fortuna no llegan de manera fácil y los contratiempos que enfrentas son parte del proceso. Aprende de tus experiencias y verás lo bien que te va en el próximo año zodiacal que comenzará a regir el próximo día 20 de marzo con el equinoccio de 2019. Biorritmo astral de hoy  Nivel de energía sexual este lunes: moderado.  Dinámica cósmica que debes aprovechar: un encuentro que puede cambiar tus planes de esta semana.  Tendencia peligrosa de hoy lunes en tu signo Escorpión: dejarte influir negativamente por un comentario relacionado con alguien que amas.  ¿Qué debo evitar?: meterte donde no te han llamado, dar opiniones que no te han pedido, la intromisión.  Predicción de pareja para hoy lunes  La mejor relación de hoy: este lunes las cosas marcharán muy bien para ti si tu pareja es de un signo de agua particularmente Cáncer o Piscis. Podría haber buena compatibilidad con Tauro y Virgo.  La relación más tensa: no se aprecian muchas tensiones, pero evita confrontaciones con nativos de Aries o Sagitario.  Tu compatibilidad actual: en general hay una buena onda de compatibilidad, pero mejor con signos de agua y de tierra.  Si estás soltero o soltera: tendrás noticias de alguien que desde hace tiempo te está buscando y ahora finalmente te encuentra, algo que será más evidente después del equinoccio de marzo cuando comience el nuevo año zodiacal.

results.actual_text: former aide has revealed for the first time how he knew the union between her and Charles was falling apart from the calendars of their engagements. As the rocky couple increasingly grew
results.plain_text: Revealed: The royal datebooks that charted the breakdown of Charles and Diana's marriage and how she used 'duty and beauty' to turn herself into a global star  Diana's former private secretary Patrick Jephson revisits his archives He reveals how she and Charles quickly went their separate ways Jephson explains how Diana went on the 'charm offensive' during public duties Diana turned herself into a global superstar by combining 'duty and beauty' Diana deliberately opted for controversial charities to support which put her out of favour with the Queen Mr Jephson left Diana after her controversial Panorama interview with Martin Bashir As her Private Secretary he was one of her closest confidantes and witnessed first-hand the disintegration of her marriage. Now Princess Diana's former aide has revealed for the first time how he knew the union between her and Charles was falling apart from the calendars of their engagements. As the rocky couple increasingly grew apart Patrick Jephson said the number of events they did together dwindled. Opening up the royal datebooks for the cameras of Channel 4 show The Royal House of Windsor, Mr Jephson explains: 'When they were first married they did most things together, but as their marriage started to get into trouble the asterisks tended to reduce in number'. Diana's solution to her marital woes was to turn herself into a global star by combining 'duty and beauty' says Mr Jephson, adding: 'British ambassadors, British enterprise saw Diana as a powerful new weapon for UK PLC, an international face for a traditional institution, combining the best of all worlds. Duty and beauty. 'She knew, that they knew, she was a political asset to diplomacy.' However, Diana deliberately opted for controversial charities to support, said Mr Jephson, which put her out of favour with the Queen. Charles and Diana's marriage struggled under the pressure of her popularity and they quickly went their separate ways When the couple were first married they attended royal events together but as their marriage crumbled things changed, according to her former private secretary Patrick Jephson Patrick Jephson, pictured behind the Princess, was Diana's private secretary from 1989 to 1996, and has spoken out in the past about his feelings towards the princess It has been 20 years since Mr Jephson last saw the private papers he donated to the Churchill Archives and for him they still 'stir up emotions'. He was the Royal Highness of Wales' private secretary from 1989 to 1996 and one of her closest confidantes. The calendars he refers to depict entries from 1989, eight years after they first wed and six years before they officially divorced. Five years into their marriage, problems had already surfaced and their differences began to damage their relationship, both in private and in public. Jephson explains how they always attended events together but it wasn't long before they went their separate ways. 'It is quite a sentimental experience looking at some of these papers. I'm surprised by how much emotion it still has the power to stir up.' The Channel 4 documentary follows Charles and Diana's marriage from beginning until the tumultuous end, charting how both parties went against the royal rule book to discredit one another by revealing details of affairs. It claims that Diana went on a charm offensive after the couple separated, with the help of the nineties catapulting her into the celebrity stratosphere, meaning she was able to 'maximise every opportunity'. The Channel 4 documentary The Royal House of Windsor looks at the life of Diana, and how she used her fame to 'maximise opportunity' Diana's humanitarian work caused her to lose favour with the Queen, who Jephson claimed she was advised to choose something less controversial The programme explains how she began 'exploiting global appeal for personal and political ends'. Mr Jephson reveals how Diana took every opportunity to turn herself into the star attraction at public events and high profile trips abroad. She drew attention to her humanitarian work by deliberately opting for controversial topics like The Leprosy Mission and the National Aids Trust - something which was said to put her out of favour with the Queen. Diana's star never waned, recalls Mr Jephson: 'British ambassadors, British enterprise saw Diana as a powerful new weapon for UK PLC, an international face for a traditional institution, combining the best of all worlds. Duty and beauty. 'She knew, that they knew, she was a political asset to diplomacy.' However, he added that her charity endeavours and lack of support at home drained Diana: 'To be engaged at an emotional level placed great demands on her emotional reserves. 'There was nobody at home at the end of a long hard day saying “kick your shoes off darling, let me make you a drink, tell me all about it, you’re doing a great job”.' Jephson’s time with Diana came to an end after her infamous BBC Panorama interview in 1995 with Martin Bashir, where she talked candidly about her and her husband’s extra-marital affairs, and questioned Prince Charles’s suitability to be King. Jephson didn’t agree with how Diana portrayed herself as the victim in her marriage and within the royal family. He said: ‘I thought that was the most terrible mistake, a princess who chose to see herself as a victim and rather pathetic. I didn’t like. If you are going to grab the world’s attention you should have something good to say and she didn’t. So I felt my time was up.’ The Royal House of Windsor airs tonight on Channel 4 at 9pm Most watched News videos  Parliament cordoned off after 'firearms incident' in Westminster Speaker of the House of Commons suspends the house after explosion Commotion outside Parliament in London after reported shooting Piers Morgan pulls out ear-piece to rant on Scottish Referendum Lovers caught in the middle of the act on their balcony Racing with wolves: Driver finds several wolves racing her car Horrifying moment HGV crashes into back of a broken down car Dirty-minded contestant blows it on Wheel of Fortune Adorable deaf baby's reaction to hearing mom's voice Jaw-dropping moment enormous chicken emerges from coop Complete chaos as wild brawl breaks out inside Pennsylvania diner North Korea 'blows up US carrier' in new propaganda video

results.actual_text: over the Spanish and French navies at the Battle of Trafalgar in 1805. One of the songs the supporters were singing was, “
results.plain_text: Newcastle fans take over Trafalgar Square ahead of Carabao Cup final Newcastle United supporters have gathered in central London’s Trafalgar Square ahead of the Carabao Cup final against Manchester United on Sunday afternoon. Fans of the Magpies have travelled down from the North East in large numbers for the match, their first appearance in the final of a major cup since losing 2-0 to United in the 1998/99 FA Cup final. The 32,761 tickets allocated to the club at the 90,000-seater Wembley Stadium sold out within a week and the travelling fans have wasted no time getting acquainted with the capital. Supporters congregated in Trafalgar Square as the sun set, singing terrace chants together, drinking cans of beer and letting off smoke bombs in the club’s black and white colours. Mehrdad Ghodoussi, the club’s co-owner, posted a picture on social media of him and fellow director Jamie Reuben enjoying the festivities with their hoods up. One middle-aged fan even stripped to his boxers to enjoy a dip in one of the square’s two fountains. Other fans congregated around Nelson’s Column in the centre of the square, which commemorates Admiral Horatio Nelson’s victory over the Spanish and French navies at the Battle of Trafalgar in 1805. One of the songs the supporters were singing was, “Tell me ma, me ma, I won’t be home for tea, we’re going to Wembley” to the tune of 1950s hit Que Sera, Sera. Another chant saw an alternative rendition of gospel hymn When the Saints Go Marching In, with “Saints” replaced by “Mags”. Newcastle have enjoyed a rapid rise up the Premier League table since the arrival of Mr Ghodoussi, Mr Reuben and other new investors. Their Saudi Arabia-led consortium has funded more than £210 million of new signings since taking over the club in October 2021. Managed by Eddie Howe, Newcastle sit fifth in the Premier League, just one point behind Tottenham Hotspur in the final Champions League qualification spot. Victory over Manchester United in tomorrow’s final would secure the club a play-off place in next season’s Europa Conference League. It would be the first time Newcastle have played in Europe since the 2012/13 campaign, when they reached the quarter-final stage of the Europa League. The best videos delivered daily Watch the stories that matter, right from your inbox

results.actual_text: منظمة العمل الدولية: انخفاض طفيف لمعدلات البطالة العالمية في
results.plain_text: منظمة العمل الدولية: انخفاض طفيف لمعدلات البطالة العالمية في 2023  قالت منظمة العمل الدولية أمس الاثنين، إنه من المتوقع أن يتراجع نمو التوظيف العالمي إلى نصف معدل النمو الحالي في العام 2022، حيث قد تحول الظروف الاقتصادية غير المؤكدة دون انتعاش سوق العمل وسط استمرار ظروف جائحة كوفيد.  وذكرت المنظمة ومقرها جنيف أمس الاثنين، في تقريرها "الاستخدام العالمي والآفاق الاجتماعية: اتجاهات 2023"، إنه من المتوقع أن ينخفض نمو الوظائف العالمية إلى 1٪ في العام 2023 من 2٪ في العام 2022.  وبلغت توقعات نمو الوظائف العالمية للعام 2023 أقل من تقديرات منظمة العمل الدولية السابقة للنمو بنسبة 5ر1٪.  وتوقعت المنظمة أن يرتفع معدل البطالة العالمية بشكل طفيف في العام 2023، بنحو 3 ملايين، إلى 208 ملايين، وهو ما يتوافق مع معدل بطالة عالمي يبلغ 8ر5٪.  وقالت منظمة العمل الدولية إن الحجم المعتدل لهذه الزيادة المتوقعة يرجع إلى حد كبير إلى ضيق المعروض من العمالة في البلدان المرتفعة الدخل، مضيفة أنها ستمثل انعكاسا للانخفاض في معدلات البطالة العالمية التي سجلت بين عامي 2020 و2022.

results.actual_text: A pochi giorni dall’anniversario del sisma del 30 ott
results.plain_text: A pochi giorni dall’anniversario del sisma del 30 ottobre 2016, la scossa più forte mai registrata in Italia dopo quella del 1980 in Irpinia, ENEA presenta un brevetto per realizzare edifici ex novo “a danno zero” e per ricostruire in sicurezza i centri storici, sviluppato in collaborazione con Tekva, azienda toscana che opera in Italia e all’estero nel mercato delle opere civili, si legge sul sito ENEA. Si tratta di una piattaforma in cemento armato, alleggerita mediante tubi in vetroresina, che consente di abbattere fino all’80% gli effetti delle scosse sismiche sugli edifici, con tempi di costruzione ridotti e a costi competitivi, rispettando l’assetto urbanistico e architettonico dei centri urbani preesistenti. Oltre al vantaggio dell’isolamento sismico, il sistema offre la possibilità di utilizzare i tubi per il passaggio dei servizi (acquedotto, fognature, gas, impianti elettrico e telefonico, teleriscaldamento) rendendone semplici ed economiche l’installazione, l’ispezione e la manutenzione. “Il sistema brevettato da ENEA e Tekva consente a tecnici e amministratori locali di prendere in considerazione la possibilità di ricostruire “come era” e, laddove possibile, “dove era” e per questo può essere una soluzione efficace per la ricostruzione di centri storici al fine di conservarne la memoria storica come, ad esempio, nei casi dei comuni di Amatrice, Accumoli e Arquata del Tronto”, spiega Paolo Clemente, dirigente di ricerca dell’ENEA. “Sul basamento, di superficie anche molto grande, si possono riprodurre gli edifici preesistenti di qualsiasi tipologia e materiali e anche aggregati edilizi complessi di forma irregolare”. A livello operativo, dopo l’esecuzione dello scavo nell’area d’interesse, si realizza un basamento di cemento armato, alleggerito con tubi in vetroresina o altro materiale. All’interno del basamento, tra la parte inferiore poggiata sul terreno e quella superiore, che sosterrà le costruzioni, vengono inseriti i dispositivi di isolamento sismico al fine di ‘disaccoppiare’ il moto dell’edificio da quello del terreno. Completano l’opera pareti perimetrali connesse alle parti inferiori dei tubi e altre pareti connesse alle parti superiori ed eventuali ulteriori dispositivi di dissipazione. “La realizzazione del basamento è semplice e veloce. In generale, con l’isolamento sismico si spende di più in fondazione ma si recupera in elevazione perché le strutture sovrastanti saranno progettate e costruite per sopportare azioni sismiche molto ridotte e, pertanto, con risparmi significativi dovuti sia alla minore quantità di materiale che alla maggiore semplicità dei dettagli costruttivi. L’isolamento sismico è addirittura competitivo rispetto alle tecniche tradizionali, almeno in zone a media e alta sismicità”, conclude Clemente. Per supportare la ricostruzione nei 138 comuni danneggiati dal terremoto, in collaborazione con CNR-IGAG, INGV e Università degli studi “G. d’Annunzio” di Chieti-Pescara, ENEA ha curato la raccolta di 12 articoli scientifici dedicato alla stima della pericolosità sismica del territorio per lo Speciale del Bulletin of Earthquake Engeneering, dal titolo “Seismic Microzonation of Central Italy following the 2016-2017 Seismic Sequence” Issue editors Salomon Hailemikael Sara Amoroso Iolanda Gaudiosi (Vol. 18, Issue 12, Springer – https://link.springer.com/journal/10518/volumes-and-issues/18-12). Gli studi sono stati realizzati nell’ambito delle attività per la Microzonazione sismica dell’Italia centrale coordinata dal Centro per la Microzonazione Sismica (CentroMS) e finanziata dal Commissario di Governo per la ricostruzione, che ha coinvolto 114 gruppi di professionisti e oltre 100 ricercatori, tra cui gli esperti dell’ENEA. Il CentroMS raccoglie i maggiori enti di ricerca e dipartimenti universitari con l’obiettivo di fornire supporto scientifico e tecnico ai soggetti interessati alla microzonazione sismica e alle sue applicazioni, alla pianificazione urbanistica e alle problematiche geologiche, geotecniche e geofisiche connesse all’emergenza sismica. “Le caratteristiche geologico-geotecniche dei siti possono concorrere a modificare sensibilmente lo scuotimento sismico atteso alla fondazione e determinare altri effetti indesiderati come frane, cedimenti, liquefazione del terreno”, spiega Salomon Hailemikael del Laboratorio Tecnologie per la DInamica delle Strutture e la PREVenzione del rischio sismico e idrogeologico dell’ENEA, e per questo la microzonazione sismica è di fondamentale importanza per ottenere una vera e propria “fotografia” delle aree a maggior pericolosità, utile per la pianificazione e la ricostruzione in sicurezza”. Brevetti e studi dell’ENEA si inseriscono nel contesto delle attività di ricerca e sviluppo tecnologico realizzate dall’Agenzia per la PA, le imprese e i cittadini al fine di garantire: supporto nella valutazione di politiche, piani e strategie per l’adattamento e la mitigazione dei rischi derivanti da cause naturali e antropiche, con particolare riferimento ad eventi estremi, come quelli sismici; modelli e sistemi per l’analisi e per lo sviluppo di scenari di valutazione degli impatti antropici e la riduzione dei rischi naturali sia in ambito locale sia su scala nazionale per interventi di risanamento e di policy; tecnologie antisismiche innovative ad edifici civili ed industriali, al patrimonio storico-culturale e monumentale.
